item.title: Re: Crosslink suggestion
item.content:encoded:
As per the Site Rules, anything that isn't correcting grammar, formatting or other errors do require permission from the author (or rewrite team) to change, so you're right in asking for permission here.

The fastest and easiest way to reach the author is probably through a wikidot PM.

item.title: Crosslink suggestion
item.content:encoded:
I've been looking through pages for anything having to do with MTF β-7, and I have a suggestion for a crosslink in the Manifestation Event 37 dropdown, in the line:

The radio crackles to life and Captain Myers informs the task force of an incoming gas attack.

This is in reference to MTF β-7's failed mission under SCP-2133.

Site Command: Felsenstein? We lost you hours ago. Where's Myers? We need a status update.

I haven't made a crosslink before, so I don't know if I have to request permission to add such a small thing like this, or if this forum is the right place to ask in. But I'm playing it safe and asking you for the greenlight, OP. edit:typo

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What happens if you fit like, multiple criteria fro 4771-B instances fro MULTIPLE animals…we don't have pictures but…. Zeus, Donovan, Pj, Chris, Tiana, Chris, crap with bad memory I can't remember all the good boys and girls we lost. wasn't super close to all of them but….even the ones like Maximilian who I found annoying I felt bad if I swatted them a little too hard when they were bad and still comforted as they died. Hell, EVERY dog or cat we have owned we have given a proper burial, at least one (Zeus) I tried to save by CPR, and all the ones that died in their sleep or slowly of disease we pet and cuddled so they weren't alone, or had in a bed. Q.Q

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ok I was just doing research to make sure my new SCP wasn't already covered, so was not prepared for this.
Your entry hit me hard.
You know that, breath catches in your throat, I'm not crying your crying, feeling. You got me to feel that for the first time in years.
Manifestation Event 37… I just….. I can't.
If writing and art's purpose is to elicit thoughts, feelings and emotions unexplored, You just nailed it.
Much deserved positive rating.
I tip my hat to you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Don't think I've posted in this website in literal years, but the impact this article had on me compelled me to post. I've been a dog owner all my life, and my 15 year old beagle is starting to slow down… I worry I'll lose her soon, and I remember how much my first dog broke my heart when she died. Reading the addenda in this article literally put me in tears and I had to share it with everyone I was talking to while I was reading it.

It's been a while since I read an article that actually evoked a visceral, real-life involuntary physical reaction in me, and this one managed it before I even got to the end. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Female Labradoodle, deceased at age 12.

Oh, this made me ugly cry. I lost my beloved dog recently, about two months ago. She was a Labradoodle too, died at 8 due to Kidney Failure.

That whole bit of a MTF leader going through rough times only to be met with his dog one last time is so touching to me.

A well deserved +1. Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: cool, an scp designed to make people cry

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shouldn't the whistle be SCP-4771-1 and the dog be SCP-4771-2? Apart from that it is a cool idea and definitely a +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can now claim that my writing was so bad I made someone cry kek.

I hope whatever you are going through, you are able to overcome :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Extenuating life circumstances are involved, but I'm a sniffling mess right now. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is wholesome and I thoroughly enjoyed it.

item.title: Thanks for Reading!
item.content:encoded:
Image Attribution: Turbojet [CC BY-SA 3.0 (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0)]

Link to Image: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Dog_ultrasound_whistle_ID_tag.jpg

Thank you to DrChandra, SrGunk, Dr. MacLeod, and LittleFieryOne for providing me with feedback on the draft!
